title: Rivian Stock Plunges on Flat 2024 Production Outlook
text: Rivian Automotive's stock dropped 25.6% following disappointing 2024 production guidance that essentially matches 2023 output levels. The company projects producing 57,000 vehicles in 2024, identical to 2023's 57,232 units. Management attributed this cautious stance to economic and geopolitical uncertainties and pressures, most notably the impact of historically high interest rates. Despite Q4 results meeting expectations, the guidance miss drove the selloff. Fourth-quarter revenue reached $1.32 billion, exceeding analyst estimates of $1.26 billion, though adjusted losses slightly exceeded projections at $1.36 per share versus expected $1.32. Fourth-quarter vehicles produced reached 17,541 units, up 8% sequentially. Operating loss narrowed 12% year-over-year to $1.58 billion. Cash position of $9.37 billion at year-end supports approximately 1.5 years of operations at current burn rates. Management announced a 10% salaried workforce reduction aimed at improving efficiency and reducing expenses. The company expects negative $2.7 billion in adjusted EBITDA for 2024, though management projects achieving modest gross profit in Q4 2024.
